description: Adversarial: a Class B artifact whose two declared replays of the same execution produce divergent observed_result fields under identical inputs MUST receive CONFLICTED. Replay drift is contradictory evidence and MUST be preserved as such (B2), never silently reconciled.

input.claim: this execution occurred with the declared observed result (two replays asserted)

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 §3 Class B and invariant B2: a conformant implementation MUST preserve contradictory evidence if encountered. Two replays under identical input_digest produce divergent observed_result_digest values. The verifier MUST NOT silently pick one and discard the other; it MUST emit CONFLICTED and retain both observations. Mitigates a replay-drift attack where an instrumented system reports a 'successful' replay alongside a discarded 'failed' replay to obtain SUPPORTED status. Exercises B1 (sources declared explicitly), B2 (contradiction preservation), and B3 (auditable evidence ordering).
